docs.title: 📚 Meta App Chatbot Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for the Meta App Chatbot. This guide provides everything you need to build, deploy, and extend your own high-concurrency WhatsApp AI agent.

Agentic Framework

The Meta App Chatbot is not just a chatbot—it's an agentic framework. It combines advanced reasoning, persistent memory, and scalable knowledge.

docs.title: 🧭 Navigation
docs.text:
• Architecture & Core Logic
  • Learn about the Multi-Agent system, RAG implementation, and the reasoning loop.
  • Configuration Guide
  • Detailed breakdown of LLM settings, Meta API credentials, and Database configurations.
  • Deployment & Setup
  • Step-by-step guide on how to go from local development to production on Google Cloud Run.
  • Tool Development
  • How to create custom tools to give your agent new superpowers.

docs.title: 🌟 Quick Overview
docs.text:
The framework is built on four core pillars:

  1. Reasoning: Cutting-edge models like GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0.
  2. Memory: Persistent conversation state in Firestore.
  3. Knowledge: Scalable domain knowledge via BigQuery RAG.
  4. Action: Real-world integration via modular tools (Odoo CRM, Meta Messaging).

docs.title: 📬 Getting Support
docs.text:
If you encounter issues or have questions:

  1. Check the Configuration Guide for common setup errors.
  2. Review the Architecture to understand state management.
  3. Open an issue on GitHub.

docs.title: 📖 Complete Project Reference
docs.text: This document serves as a comprehensive guide to the Meta App Chatbot. For specific sections, you can also refer to the individual guides in the docs/ folder.

docs.title: 🗺️ Documentation Map
docs.text:
• 🏗️ Architecture: Deep dive into reasoning loops and tools.
  • ⚙️ Configuration: Full settings reference.
  • 🚀 Deployment: How to launch in production.

docs.title: 🌟 Project Vision
docs.text: The goal of this project is to provide a production-ready, highly extensible AI Agent for Meta platforms (WhatsApp, Facebook). It is built to handle thousands of concurrent conversations while maintaining a "brain" that can access company data (RAG) and perform real-world tasks (Tools).

docs.title: 🛠️ Core Stack
docs.text:
• Logic: FastAPI (Async/High Performance)
  • Intelligence: Google Gemini & OpenAI GPT-⅘
  • Knowledge Base: Google BigQuery (Vector RAG)
  • State Management: Google Firestore
  • Configuration: Dynaconf

docs.title: 🧬 Data Flow Architecture
docs.text:
1. Incoming: Meta Webhook (WhatsApp/FB/IG Message)
  2. Dispatcher: FastAPI Router extracts text/audio and sender info.
  3. State Manager: Firestore retrieves the recent message history.
  4. Knowledge Retrieval: BigQuery finds relevant facts based on user query.
  5. Reasoning: LLM analyzes context and decides to:
  6. Call a Tool (e.g., Odoo search, Shipping tracking).
  7. Formulate a direct reply.
  8. Action: Outbound message sent via Meta Cloud API.

docs.title: 🤝 Open Source & Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Please see our main README for guidelines on how to submit pull requests.

Made with ❤️ for the AI Community

docs.title: 🏗️ Architecture & Core Logic
docs.text:
The Meta App Chatbot is designed as a Modular Agent Framework. Unlike simple request-response bots, it uses a reasoning loop to decide how to handle each message.

Design Principle

The framework favors composition over inheritance. Tools, agents, and databases are all hot-swappable via the configuration layer.

docs.title: 1. The Main Agent Orchestrator
docs.text:
The Main Agent is the brain of the system. Its primary responsibilities include:

  • Intent Extraction: Understanding what the user wants.
  • Context Management: Retrieving historical messages from Firestore to maintain conversation flow.
  • Tool Dispatching: Selecting the right tool for the job.

docs.title: 2. Multi-LLM Model Factory
docs.text:
The system is model-agnostic. Via the Model Factory, you can swap between:

  • OpenAI: GPT-4o, o1.
  • Google Gemini: Gemini 1.5 Pro, 2.0 Flash.
  • Vertex AI: Enterprise-grade Google Cloud LLMs.

docs.title: 3. BigQuery RAG Pipeline
docs.text:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) empowers the agent with domain-specific knowledge without fine-tuning.

  • Indexing: Data is stored in BigQuery tables.
  • Retrieval: Fetches relevant context based on vector similarity.
  • Injection: Context is added to the system prompt as "Ground Truth Facts."

docs.title: 🛠️ Extending Tools
docs.text:
Adding a new tool is straightforward. A tool is a Python function decorated with @tool.

PythonRegistration 
@tool
def check_inventory(product_name: str) -> str:
    """Useful for checking if a product is in stock."""
    # Your logic here (e.g., API call to Odoo or Database)
    return f"We have 10 units of {product_name} in stock."

Tools are automatically discovered if they are in the agent/tools directory and properly imported.

docs.title: 🔄 The Reasoning Loop
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Webhook Message] --> B{State Manager}
    B --> C[Retrieve History]
    C --> D[RAG Lookup]
    D --> E[LLM Reasoning]
    E --> F{Tool Needed?}
    F -- Yes --> G[Execute Tool]
    G --> E
    F -- No --> H[Final Response]
    H --> I[Meta Outbound API]

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuration Guide
docs.text:
The project uses Dynaconf to manage hierarchical settings.

Security Reminder

Never commit your settings.toml with real secrets to public repositories. Use environment variables or secret managers in production.

docs.title: 📄 settings.toml Reference
docs.text: All settings are located in meta_app_chatbot/config/settings.toml.

docs.title: 🤖 LLM Strategy
docs.text: Key Default Description main_agent_model gpt-4o-mini The workhorse model for complex reasoning. tool_agent_model gemini-2.0-flash The faster model used for tool identification. PLATFORM openai Default provider (openai, gemini, vertex).

docs.title: 📱 Meta API
docs.text: Key Description WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN Your Meta System User Access Token. WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID The ID of your WhatsApp phone number. VERIFY_TOKEN Custom string used to verify your webhook.

docs.title: 🌍 Environment Variables
docs.text:
Override any .toml setting by prefixing with DYNACONF_.

Example: Override API Key
export DYNACONF_OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

docs.title: 🔒 Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Template First: Use settings.toml.example as a template for team members.
  2. Secret Manager: Use Google Secret Manager for production keys.
  3. Volume Mounts: In Docker, mount service account keys instead of baking them into the image.

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing to Meta App Chatbot
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to the Meta App Chatbot! We welcome all contributions, including bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, and code changes.

docs.title: 1. 🐛 Reporting Bugs
docs.text:
If you find a bug, please create a new issue on GitHub. Include:

  • A clear, descriptive title.
  • Steps to reproduce the bug.
  • Actual vs. expected behavior.
  • Any relevant logs or screenshots.

docs.title: 2. ✨ Feature Requests
docs.text:
Have an idea to make the chatbot better? Open an issue and describe:

  • The problem it solves.
  • How you envision it working.

docs.title: 3. 🛠️ Code Contributions
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository and clone it locally.

  2. Install development dependencies:

    uv sync --dev

  3. Create a new branch for your feature or fix.

  4. Write code and tests.

  5. Run lints and tests before submitting:

    ruff check .
pytest

  6. Push to your fork and open a Pull Request to the main branch.

docs.title: 📜 Code Style
docs.text: We use Ruff for linting and formatting. Ensure your code follows the project's standards by running ruff check . --fix.

docs.title: 📄 License
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the project's MIT License.

docs.title: 🚀 Deployment & Setup
docs.text: This guide will help you deploy the Meta App Chatbot to a production environment.

docs.title: 🏗️ Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Meta Developer Account: Create an app and set up the WhatsApp/Facebook products.
  • Google Cloud Project: Enabled with BigQuery and Firestore.
  • Docker: Installed locally for building the image.

docs.title: 🛠️ Local Development
docs.text:
Step 1: CloneStep 2: EnvironmentStep 3: Run 
git clone https://github.com/mohamed-em2m/meta-bot.git
cd meta-bot

uv sync
source .venv/bin/activate

python meta_app_chatbot/main.py

docs.title: 🐳 Docker Deployment
docs.text:
The provided Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build to ensure a slim runtime image.

Build and Run
# Build the image
docker build -t meta-chatbot .

# Run the container locally
docker run -p 8080:8080 --env-file .env meta-chatbot

docs.title: ☁️ Google Cloud Run
docs.text:
Recommended Pattern

We recommend using Google Cloud Run for its seamless integration with Firestore and BigQuery and its ability to scale to zero.

  1. Push to Artifact Registry:

    docker tag meta-chatbot gcr.io/[PROJECT_ID]/meta-chatbot
docker push gcr.io/[PROJECT_ID]/meta-chatbot

  2. Deploy Command:

    gcloud run deploy meta-chatbot \
  --image gcr.io/[PROJECT_ID]/meta-chatbot \
  --platform managed \
  --port 8080

docs.title: 📬 Webhook Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common Issues
  • Verification Failed: Ensure your VERIFY_TOKEN matches exactly.
  • Timeout (500 Error): Meta requires a response within 3 seconds. The project handles this via background tasks.
  • Permissions: Ensure your Meta App has messages and messaging_postbacks enabled.
